isQuietHours() bool {\n303\t\ts.mu.RLock()\n304\t\tqh := s.quietHours\n305\t\ts.mu.RUnlock()\n306\t\n307\t\tif qh == nil || !qh.Enabled {\n308\t\t\treturn false\n309\t\t}\n310\t\n311\t\tnow := time.Now()\n312\t\tcurrentMins := now.Hour()*60 + now.Minute()\n313\t\tstartMins := qh.StartHour*60 + qh.StartMin\n314\t\tendMins := qh.EndHour*60 + qh.EndMin\n315\t\n316\t\tif startMins < endMins {\n317\t\t\t// Quiet hours don't cross midnight\n318\t\t\treturn currentMins >= startMins && currentMins < endMins\n319\t\t}\n320\t\t// Quiet hours cross midnight\n321\t\treturn currentMins >= startMins || currentMins < endMins\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// Send sends a notification to all enabled channels.\n325\t// URGENT and Critical priority notifications bypass quiet hours.\n326\tfunc (s *Service) Send(notif Notification) error {\n327\t\tif s.isQuietHours() && notif.Priority < int(PriorityUrgent) {\n328\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Notification suppressed (quiet hours): %s\", notif.Title)\n329\t\t\treturn nil\n330\t\t}\n331\t\n332\t\ts.mu.RLock()\n333\t\tchannels := make([]*ChannelConfig, 0, len(s.channels))\n334\t\tfor _, cc := range s.channels {\n335\t\t\tchannels = append(channels, cc)\n336\t\t}\n337\t\ts.mu.RUnlock()\n338\t\n339\t\tif len(channels) == 0 {\n340\t\t\treturn nil\n341\t\t}\n342\t\n343\t\tvar lastErr error\n344\t\tfor _, cc := range channels {\n345\t\t\tif !cc.Enabled {\n346\t\t\t\tcontinue\n347\t\t\t}\n348\t\n349\t\t\tvar err error\n350\t\t\tswitch cc.Type {\n351\t\t\tcase ChannelNtfy:\n352\t\t\t\terr = s.sendNtfy(cc, notif)\n353\t\t\tcase ChannelPushover:\n354\t\t\t\terr = s.sendPushover(cc, notif)\n355\t\t\tcase ChannelGotify:\n356\t\t\t\terr = s.sendGotify(cc, notif)\n357\t\t\tcase ChannelWebhook:\n358\t\t\t\terr = s.sendWebhook(cc, notif)\n359\t\t\tdefault:\n360\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Unknown notification channel type: %s\", cc.Type)\n361\t\t\t\tcontinue\n362\t\t\t}\n363\t\n364\t\t\tsuccess := err == nil\n365\t\t\tif s.onSend != nil {\n366\t\t\t\ts.onSend(string(cc.Type), notif, success)\n367\t\t\t}\n368\t\n369\t\t\ts.recordHistory(string(cc.Type), notif, success, err)\n370\t\n371\t\t\tif err != nil {\n372\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to send notification via %s: %v\", cc.Type, err)\n373\t\t\t\tlastErr = err\n374\t\t\t} else {\n375\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Notification sent via %s: %s\", cc.Type, notif.Title)\n376\t\t\t}\n377\t\t}\n378\t\n379\t\treturn lastErr\n380\t}\n381\t\n382\t// SendBatched queues a notification for batch sending.\n383\tfunc (s *Service) SendBatched(notif Notification, channels []string) error {\n384\t\ts.mu.Lock()\n385\t\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n386\t\n387\t\ts.pending = append(s.pending, batchedNotification{\n388\t\t\tnotif:    notif,\n389\t\t\tchannels: channels,\n390\t\t})\n391\t\n392\t\t// Start batch timer if not running\n393\t\tif s.batchTimer == nil {\n394\t\t\ts.batchTimer = time.AfterFunc(s.batchWindow, s.flushBatch)\n395\t\t}\n396\t\n397\t\treturn nil\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\tfunc (s *Service) flushBatch() {\n401\t\ts.mu.Lock()\n402\t\tpending := s.pending\n403\t\ts.pending = nil\n404\t\ts.batchTimer = nil\n405\t\ts.mu.Unlock()\n406\t\n407\t\tif len(pending) == 0 {\n408\t\t\treturn\n409\t\t}\n410\t\n411\t\t// Merge notifications by title\n412\t\tmerged := make(map[string]*Notification)\n413\t\tfor _, p := range pending {\n414\t\t\tif existing, exists := merged[p.notif.Title]; exists {\n415\t\t\t\t// Append body\n416\t\t\t\texisting.Body += \"\\n\" + p.notif.Body\n417\t\t\t} else {\n418\t\t\t\tmerged[p.notif.Title] = &p.notif\n419\t\t\t}\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\t// Send merged notifications\n423\t\tfor _, notif := range merged {\n424\t\t\ts.Send(*notif)\n425\t\t}\n426\t}\n427\t\n428\t// recordHistory records notification send attempt in history.\n429\tfunc (s *Service) recordHistory(channel string, notif Notification, success bool, err error) {\n430\t\terrMsg := \"\"\n431\t\tif err != nil {\n432\t\t\terrMsg = err.Error()\n433\t\t}\n434\t\n435\t\t_, dbErr := s.db.Exec(`\n436\t\t\tINSERT INTO notification_history (channel, title, body, success, error_msg, timestamp)\n437\t\t\tVALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n438\t\t`, channel, notif.Title, notif.Body, success, errMsg, time.Now().UnixNano())\n439\t\tif dbErr != nil {\n440\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to record notification history: %v\", dbErr)\n441\t\t}\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// sendNtfy sends notification via ntfy.sh.\n445\tfunc (s *Service) sendNtfy(cc *ChannelConfig, notif Notification) error {\n446\t\tif cc.URL == \"\" {\n447\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ntfy URL not configured\")\n448\t\t}\n449\t\n450\t\treq, err := http.NewRequest(\"POST\", cc.URL, bytes.NewBufferString(notif.Body))\n451\t\tif err != nil {\n452\t\t\treturn err\n453\t\t}\n454\t\n455\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Title\", notif.Title)\n456\t\tif len(notif.Tags) > 0 {\n457\t\t\ttags := \"\"\n458\t\t\tfor _, t := range notif.Tags {\n459\t\t\t\tif tags != \"\" {\n460\t\t\t\t\ttags += \",\"\n461\t\t\t\t}\n462\t\t\t\ttags += t\n463\t\t\t}\n464\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Tags\", tags)\n465\t\t}\n466\t\tif notif.Priority > 0 {\n467\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Priority\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", notif.Priority))\n468\t\t}\n469\t\n470\t\t// Attach image if available\n471\t\tif len(notif.Image) > 0 && notif.ImageType != \"\" {\n472\t\t\t// For ntfy, we attach as base64 in header\n473\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Image\", \"data:\"+notif.ImageType+\";base64,\"+base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(notif.Image))\n474\t\t}\n475\t\n476\t\tif cc.Username != \"\" || cc.Password != \"\" {\n477\t\t\treq.SetBasicAuth(cc.Username, cc.Password)\n478\t\t}\n479\t\n480\t\tresp, err := s.httpClient.Do(req)\n481\t\tif err != nil {\n482\t\t\treturn err\n483\t\t}\n484\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n485\t\n486\t\tif resp.StatusCode >= 400 {\n487\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ntfy returned status %d\", resp.StatusCode)\n488\t\t}\n489\t\treturn nil\n490\t}\n491\t\n492\t// sendPushover sends notification via Pushover API.\n493\tfunc (s *Service) sendPushover(cc *ChannelConfig, notif Notification) error {\n494\t\tif cc.Token == \"\" || cc.User == \"\" {\n495\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"pushover token/user not pz < 5 || pz >= height-5 {\n649\t\t\t\tcontinue\n650\t\t\t}\n651\t\n652\t\t\t// Color based on state\n653\t\t\tclr := color.RGBA{100, 181, 246, 255} // Blue - normal\n654\t\t\tif blob.IsFall {\n664\t\t\t\t\t\timg.Set(px+dx, pz+dy, clr)\n665\t\t\t\t\t}\n666\t\t\t\t}\n667\t\t\t}\n668\t\t}\n669\t\n670\t\t// Draw room outline\n671\t\toutlineColor := color.RGBA{100, 100, 120, 255}\n672\t\tfor nil, err\n685\t\t}\n686\t\n687\t\treturn buf.Bytes(), nil\n688\t}\n689\t\n690\t// GetQuietHours returns the current quiet hours configuration.\n691\tfunc (s *Service) GetQuietHours() QuietHoursConfig qh.StartMin\n\tendMins := qh.EndHour*60 + qh.EndMin\n\n\tif startMins < endMins {\n\t\t// Quiet hours don't cross midnight\n\t\treturn currentMins >= startMins && currentMins < endMins\n\t}\n\t// Quiet hours cross midnight\n\treturn currentMins >= startMins || currentMins < endMins\n}\n\n// Send sends a notification to all enabled channels.\n// URGENT and Critical priority notifications bypass quiet hours.\nfunc (s *Service) Send(notif Notification) error {\n\tif s.isQuietHours() && notif.Priority < int(PriorityUrgent) {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Notification suppressed (quiet hours): %s\", notif.Title)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\ts.mu.RLock()\n\tchannels := make([]*ChannelConfig, 0, len(s.channels))\n\tfor _, cc := range s.channels {\n\t\tchannels = append(channels, cc.Type)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsuccess := err == nil\n\t\tif s.onSend != nil {\n\t\t\ts.onSend(string(cc.Type), notif, success)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ts.recordHistory(string(cc.Type), notif, success, err)\n\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to send notification via %s: %v\", cc.Type, err)\n\t\t\tlastErr = err\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Notification sent via %s: GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail(width, height int, blobs []struct {\n\tX, Y, Z float64\n\tIdentity string\n\tIsFall   bool\n}) ([]byte, error) {\n\ts.mu.RLock()\n\troomConfig := s.roomConfig\n\tfloorPlan 46, 255}}, image.Point{}, draw.Src)\n\n\t// Get room dimensions\n\tvar roomW, roomD float64 = 6.0, 5.0 // defaults\n\tif roomConfig != nil {\n\t\troomW, _, roomD = roomConfig.GetRoom()\n\t}\n\n\t// Scale factor\n\tscaleX := float64(width) / roomW\n\tscaleZ := float64(height) / roomD\n\n\t// Draw floor plan if available\n\tif len(floorPlan) > 0 {\n\t\t// Would decode and draw, but skip for simplicity\n\t}\n\n\t// Draw blobs\n\tfor _, blob := range blobs {\n\t\tpx := int(blob.X * scaleX)\n\t\tpz := int(blob.Z * scaleZ)\n\n\t\t// Clamp to image bounds\n\t\tif px < 5 || px >= width-5 || pz < 5 || pz >= height-5 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Color based on state\n\t\tclr := color.RGBA{100, 181, 246, 255} // Blue - normal\n\t\tif blob.IsFall {\n\t\t\tclr = color.RGBA{239, 83, 80, 255} // Red - fall detected\n\t\t} else if blob.Identity != \"\" {\n\t\t\tclr = color.RGBA{129, 199, 132, 255} // Green - identified\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Draw circle\n\t\tfor dy := -4; dy <= 4; dy++ {\n\t\t\tfor dx := -4; dx <= 4; dx++ {\n\t\t\t\tif dx*dx+dy*dy <= 16 {\n\t\t\t\t\timg.Set(px+dx, pz+dy, clr)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Draw room outline\n\toutlineColor := color.RGBA{100, 100, 120, 255}\n\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n\t\timg.Set(x, 0, outlineColor)\n\t\timg.Set(x, height-1, outlineColor)\n\t}\n\tfor y := 0; y < height; y++ {\n\t\timg.Set(0, y, outlineColor)\n\t\timg.Set(width-1, y, outlineColor)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encode to PNG\n\tvar buf bytes.Buffer\n\tif err := png.Encode(&buf, img); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn today\n\ttodayDate := time.Now().Format(\"2006-01-02\")\n\tif ext.digestSentToday && ext.digestLastDate == todayDate {\n\t\text.mu.Unlock()\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tqueued := ext.queuedDuringQuiet\n\text.queuedDuringQuiet = nil\n\text.digestSentToday = true\n\text.digestLastDate = todayDate\n\text.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif len(queued) == 0 {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create digest notification\n\tvar bodies []string\n\n\tfor _, n := range queued {\n\t\tbodies = append(bodies, n.Body)\n\t}\n\n\ttitle := fmt.Sprintf(\"Morning digest - %d events while you slept\", len(queued))\n\tbody := fmt.Sprintf(\"While you were asleep: %s\", strings.Join(bodies, \". \"))\n\n\tdigestNotif := Notification{\n\t\tTitle:     title,\n\t\tBody:      body,\n\t\tPriority:  int(PriorityLow),\n\t\tTags:      []string{\"digest\", \"morning\"},\n\t\tData: map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"event_count\": len(queued),\n\t\t},\n\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Send digest immediately\n\text.Service.Send(digestNotif)\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Morning digest sent with %d events\", len(queued))\n}\n\n// GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended generates a mini floor plan PNG with zone positions.\nfunc (ext *ExtendedService) GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended(width, height int, zones []struct {\n\tID, Name, Color string\n\tX, Y, W, D      float64\n\tHighlight       bool\n}, people []struct {\n\tName, Color string\n\tX, Y, Z     float64\n\tConfidence  float64\n\tIsFall      bool\n}) ([]byte, error) {\n\t// Create image\n\timg := image.NewRGBA(image.Rect(0, 0, width, height))\n\n\t// Fill background\n\tdraw.Draw(img, img.Bounds(), &image.Uniform{color.RGBA{26, 26, 46, 255}}, image.Point{}, draw.Src)\n\n\t// Get room dimensions from room config provider\n\tvar roomW, roomD float64 = 6.0, 5.0\n\n\t// Scale factor\n\tmargin := 10\n\tdrawW := float64(width - 2*margin)\n\tdrawH := float64(height - 2*margin)\n\tscaleX := drawW / roomW\n\tscaleZ := drawH / roomD\n\tscale := scaleX\n\tif scaleZ < scale {\n\t\tscale = scaleZ\n\t}\n\n\toffsetX := float64(margin) + (drawW-roomW*scale)/2\n\toffsetY := float64(margin) + (drawH-roomD*scale)/2\n\n\t// Draw zones\n\tfor _, zone := range zones {\n\t\tx := int(offsetX + zone.X*scale)\n\t\ty := int(offsetY + zone.Y*scale)\n\t\tw := int(zone.W * scale)\n\t\th := int(zone.D * scale)\n\n\t\t// Use NRGBA (non-premultiplied) for semi-transparent colors.\n\t\t// color.RGBA stores premultiplied alpha, which causes overflow artifacts\n\t\t// when the stored R/G/B values exceed the alpha value.\n\t\tzoneColor := color.NRGBA{79, 195, 247, 51} // Default blue with 20% opacity\n\t\tif zone.Highlight {\n\t\t\tzoneColor = color.NRGBA{79, 195, 247, 150} // Brighter for highlight\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Draw zone fill\n\t\tdraw.Draw(img, image.Rect(x, y, x+w, y+h), &image.Uniform{zoneColor}, image.Point{}, draw.Over)\n\n\t\t// Draw outline\n\t\tvar outlineColor color.Color = color.NRGBA{255, 255, 255, 100}\n\t\tif zone.Highlight {\n\t\t\toutlineColor = color.RGBA{255, 255, 255, 255}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Draw outline (simplified - just corners)\n\t\timg.Set(x, y, outlineColor)\n\t\timg.Set(x+w-1, y, outlineColor)\n\t\timg.Set(x, y+h-1, outlineColor)\n\t\timg.Set(x+w-1, y+h-1, outlineColor)\n\t}\n\n\t// Draw people\n\tfor _, person := range people {\n\t\tpx := int(offsetX + person.X*scale)\n\t\tpy := int(offsetY + person.Y*scale)\n\n\t\t// Clamp to image bounds\n\t\tif px < 5 || px >= width-5 || py < 5 || py >= height-5 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Color based on state\n\t\tclr := color.RGBA{100, 181, 246, 255} // Blue - normal\n\t\tif person.IsFall {\n\t\t\tclr = color.RGBA{239, 83, 80, 255} // Red - fall detected\n\t\t} else if person.Color != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Parse hex color\n\t\t\tfmt.Sscanf(person.Color, \"#%02x%02x%02x\", &clr.R, &clr.G, &clr.B)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Draw circle\n\t\tdiameter := 10\n\t\tfor dy := -diameter / 2; dy <= diameter/2; dy++ {\n\t\t\tfor dx := -diameter / 2; dx <= diameter/2; dx++ {\n\t\t\t\tif dx*dx+dy*dy <= (diameter/2)*(diameter/2) {\n\t\t\t\t\tpx2 := px + dx\n\t\t\t\t\tpy2 := py + dy\n\t\t\t\t\tif px2 >= 0 && px2 < width && py2 >= 0 && py2 < height {\n\t\t\t\t\t\timg.Set(px2, py2, clr)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Encode to PNG\n\tvar buf bytes.Buffer\n\tif err := png.Encode(&buf, img); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn buf.Bytes(), nil\n}\n\n// GetHistoryExtended returns recent notification history with type information.\nfunc (ext *ExtendedService) GetHistoryExtended(limit int) []struct {\n\tChannel   string\n\tType      string\n\tTitle     string\n\tBody      string\n\tSuccess   bool\n\tError     string\n\tTimestamp time.Time\n} {\n\t// Use the base Service's GetHistory method\n\tbaseHistory := ext.Service.GetHistory(limit)\n\n\t// Convert to extended format\n\tvar history []struct {\n\t\tChannel   string\n\t\tType      string\n\t\tTitle     string\n\t\tBody      string\n\t\tSuccess   bool\n\t\tError     string\n\t\tTimestamp time.Time\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, h := range baseHistory {\n\t\thistory = append(history, struct {\n\t\t\tChannel   string\n\t\t\tType      string\n\t\t\tTitle     string\n\t\t\tBody      string\n\t\t\tSuccess   bool\n\t\t\tError     string\n\t\t\tTimestamp h.Success,\n\t\t\tError:     h.Error,\n\t\t\tTimestamp: h.Timestamp,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn history\n}\n\n// SetBatchingConfig updates the batching configuration.\nfunc (ext *ExtendedService) TestZoneBoundaryRendering tests zone boundary rendering at exact pixel coordinates.\n725\tfunc TestZoneBoundaryRendering(t *testing.T) {\n726\t\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_zone_boundary.db\"\n727\t\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n728\t\tif err != nil {\n729\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n730\t\t}\n731\t\text, err := coordinates\n738\t\tzones := []struct {\n739\t\t\tID, Name, Color string\n740\t\t\tX, Y, W, D      float64\n741\t\t\tHighlight       bool\n742\t\t}{\n743\t\t\t{ID: \"z1\", Name: \"TestZone\", X: 1.0, Y: 1.0, W: 2.0, D: 2.0, Highlight: false},\n744\t\t\t{ID: \"z2\", Name: \"Highlight\", X: 3.5, Y: 3.5, W: 1.5, D: 1.5, Highlight: true},\n745\t\t}\n746\t\n747\t\tdata, err := := image.Decode(bytes.NewReader(data))\n753\t\tif err != nil {\n754\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode PNG: %v\", err)\n755\t\t}\n756\t\n757\t\t// Compute expected zone positions (same as TestFloorPlanZoneBoundaryPixels)\n758\t\tconst margin = 10\n759\t\tconst roomW = 6.0\n760\t\tconst roomD = 5.0\n761\t\timgSize := 300\n762\t\tdrawW := float64(imgSize - 2*margin)\n763\t\tdrawH := float64(imgSize - 2*margin)\n764\t\tscaleX := drawW / roomW\n765\t\tscaleZ := drawH / roomD\n766\t\tscale := scaleX\n767\t\tif scaleZ < scale {\n768\t\t\tscale = scaleZ\n769\t\t}\n770\t\toffsetX := float64(margin) + (drawW-roomW*scale)/2\n771\t\toffsetY := float64(margin) + (drawH-roomD*scale)/2\n772\t\n773\t\t// Verify zone corners are at expected positions\n774\t\t// Zone 1: X=1.0, Y=1.0, W=2.0, D=2.0\n775\t\texpectedX1 := int(offsetX + 1.0*scale)\n776\t\texpectedY1 := int(offsetY + 1.0*scale)\n777\t\texpectedX2 := int(offsetX + 3.0*scale)\n778\t\texpectedY2 := int(offsetY + 3.0*scale)\n779\t\n780\t\t// Check that zone corners are within image bounds\n781\t\tif expectedX1 < 0 || expectedX1 >= imgSize {\n782\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone 1 X1 out of bounds: %d\", expectedX1)\n783\t\t}\n784\t\tif expectedY1 < 0 || expectedY1 >= imgSize {\n785\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone 1 Y1 out of bounds: %d\", expectedY1)\n786\t\t}\n787\t\tif expectedX2 < 0 || expectedX2 >= imgSize {\n788\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone 1 X2 out of bounds: %d\", expectedX2)\n789\t\t}\n790\t\tif expectedY2 < 0 || expectedY2 >= imgSize {\n791\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone 1 Y2 out of bounds: %d\", expectedY2)\n792\t\t}\n793\t\n794\t\t// Verify highlighted zone has different color\n795\t\tcenter1X := (expectedX1 + expectedX2) / 2\n796\t\tcenter1Y := (expectedY1 + expectedY2) / 2\n797\t\tr, g, b, _ := img.At(center1X, center1Y).RGBA()\n798\t\tr8, g8, b8 := uint8(r>>8), uint8(g>>8), uint8(b>>8)\n799\t\n800\t\t// The zone fill color is NRGBA{79, 195, 247, 51} over background\n801\t\t// Should have noticeable blue component\n802\t\tif b8 < 50 {\n803\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone center expected blue-ish color, got R=%d G=%d B=%d\", r8, g8, b8)\n804\t\t}\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t// TestRendererProduces300x300PNG tests the exact output specification.\n808\tfunc TestRendererProduces300x300PNG(t *testing.T) {\n809\t\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_300x300.db\"\n810\t\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n811\t\tif err != nil {\n812\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n813\t\t}\n814\t\tdefer service.Close()\n815\t\n816\t\tdata, err := service.GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail(300, 300, nil)\n817\t\tif err != nil {\n818\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail() error = %v\", err)\n819\t\t}\n820\t\n821\t\t// Verify PNG format\n822\t\tif len(data) < 8 {\n823\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"PNG data too short: %d bytes\", len(data))\n824\t\t}\n825\t\tpngSig := []byte{0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A}\n826\t\tfor i, b := range pngSig {\n827\t\t\tif data[i] != b {\n828\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Not a PNG file (byte %d = %d, want %d)\", i, data[i], b)\n829\t\t\t}\n830\t\t}\n831\t\n832\t\t// Decode and verify dimensions\n833\t\timg, format, err := image.Decode(bytes.NewReader(data))\n834\t\tif err != nil {\n835\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode PNG: %v\", err)\n836\t\t}\n837\t\tif format != \"png\" {\n838\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format = %q, want 'png'\", format)\n839\t\t}\n840\t\n841\t\tbounds := img.Bounds()\n842\t\tif bounds.Dx() != 300 {\n843\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Width = %d, want 300\", bounds.Dx())\n844\t\t}\n845\t\tif bounds.Dy() != 300 {\n846\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Height = %d, want 300\", bounds.Dy())\n847\t\t}\n848\t\n849\t\t// Verify background color (dark gray #1a1a2e)\n850\t\t// Check a pixel inside the image, not on the edge where \"/test_pushover_ok.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\t// Replace HTTP client to intercept the hardcoded Pushover API URL\n\t// The client will redirect any request to the Pushover API to our test server\n\toriginalTransport := http.DefaultTransport\n\tservice.httpClient = &http.Client{\n\t\tTransport: roundTripperFunc(func(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {\n\t\t\t// Redirect to test server\n\t\t\tnewURL, _ := url.Parse(server.URL)\n\t\t\treq.URL = newURL\n\t\t\treq.Host = newURL.Host\n\t\t\treturn originalTransport.RoundTrip(req)\n\t\t}),\n\t}\n\n\tif err := service.AddChannel(\"po1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelPushover,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tToken:   \"aXXXXXX\",\n\t\tUser:    \"uXXXXXX\",\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddChannel() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tnotif := Notification{\n\t\tTitle:     \"Pushover Test\",\n\t\tBody:      \"Test body\",\n\t\tPriority:  int(PriorityHigh),\n\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\tif err := service.Send(notif); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Send() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif capturedFormData == nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"No form data received\")\n\t}\n\tif capturedFormData.Get(\"token\") != \"aXXXXXX\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"token = %q, want 'aXXXXXX'\", capturedFormData.Get(\"token\"))\n\t}\n\tif capturedFormData.Get(\"user\") != \"uXXXXXX\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"user = %q, want 'uXXXXXX'\", capturedFormData.Get(\"user\"))\n\t}\n\tif capturedFormData.Get(\"title\") != \"Pushover Test\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"title = %q, want 'Pushover Test'\", capturedFormData.Get(\"title\"))\n\t}\n\tif capturedFormData.Get(\"message\") != \"Test body\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"message = %q, want 'Test body'\", capturedFormData.Get(\"message\"))\n\t}\n\tif capturedFormData.Get(\"priority\") != \"3\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"priority = %q, want '3'\", capturedFormData.Get(\"priority\"))\n\t}\n}\n\n// roundTripperFunc is an http.RoundTripper that calls a function.\ntype roundTripperFunc func(*http.Request) (*http.Response, error)\n\nfunc (f roundTripperFunc) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {\n\treturn f(req)\n}\n\n// TestGotifySuccessfulDelivery tests Gotify API with successful response.\nfunc TestGotifySuccessfulDelivery(t *testing.T) {\n\tvar capturedPayload map[string]interface{}\n\tvar capturedToken string\n\n\tserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tcapturedToken = r.URL.Query().Get(\"token\")\n\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\t\tjson.Unmarshal(body, &capturedPayload)\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tfmt.Fprint(w, `{\"id\": \"123\"}`)\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_gotify_ok.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\tif err := service.AddChannel(\"gotify1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelGotify,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     server.URL,\n\t\tToken:   \"test-token\",\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddChannel() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tnotif := Notification{\n\t\tTitle:     \"Gotify Success\",\n\t\tBody:      \"Test message\",\n\t\tPriority:  int(PriorityMedium),\n\t\tTags:      []string{\"test\", \"integration\"},\n\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\tif err := service.Send(notif); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Send() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif capturedToken != \"test-token\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"token query param = %q, want 'test-token'\", capturedToken)\n\t}\n\tif capturedPayload[\"title\"] != \"Gotify Success\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"title = %v, want 'Gotify Success'\", capturedPayload[\"title\"])\n\t}\n\tif capturedPayload[\"message\"] != \"Test message\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"message = %v, want 'Test message'\", capturedPayload[\"message\"])\n\t}\n\t// JSON unmarshaling converts numbers to float64\n\tpriority, ok := capturedPayload[\"priority\"].(float64)\n\tif !ok || priority != float64(PriorityMedium) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"priority = %v (type %T), want %d\", capturedPayload[\"priority\"], capturedPayload[\"priority\"], int(PriorityMedium))\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestNtfyWithAuth tests ntfy delivery with basic authentication.\nfunc TestNtfyWithAuth(t *testing.T) {\n\tvar capturedUsername, {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\tif err := service.AddChannel(\"wh1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelWebhook,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     server.URL,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddChannel() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create a larger test PNG (100x100)\n\timg := image.NewRGBA(image.Rect(0, 0, 100, 100))\n\tfor y := 0; y < 100; y++ {\n\t\tfor x := 0; x < 100; x++ {\n\t\t\timg.Set(x, y, color.RGBA{uint8(x), uint8(y), 128, 255})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tvar buf bytes.Buffer\n\tpng.Encode(&buf, img)\n\tpngData := TestBatchingWindowExpiry tests that notifications are sent when batch window expires.\nfunc TestBatchingWindowExpiry(t *testing.T) {\n\tsentCount := 0\n\tserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tsentCount++\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_batch_window.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\tif err := base.AddChannel(\"wh1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelWebhook,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     server.URL,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddChannel() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set short batch window\n\text.SetBatchingConfig(BatchingConfig{\n\t\tEnabled:          true,\n\t\tBatchWindowSec:   1, // 1 second window\n\t\tMaxBatchSize:     100, // High max size, won't trigger flush\n\t\tBatchLowPriority: true,\n\t\tBatchMedium:      true,\n\t})\n\n\t// Queue 2 LOW priority events\n\tfor i := 0; i < 2; i++ {\n\t\tif err := ext.queueForBatching(Notification{\n\t\t\tTitle:     \"Motion detected\",\n\t\t\tBody:      fmt.Sprintf(\"Event %d\", i),\n\t\t\tPriority:  int(PriorityLow),\n\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t\t}); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"queueForBatching() error: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Wait for batch window to expire\n\ttime.Sleep(1200 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t// Should have sent 1 merged notification\n\tif sentCount != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 merged notification after batch window expiry, got %d\", sentCount)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestQuietHoursCrossMidnight tests quiet hours detection across midnight.\nfunc TestQuietHoursCrossMidnight(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_qh_midnight.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\t// Set quiet hours 22:00 - 07:00 (crosses midnight)\n\tif err := service.SetQuietHours(QuietHoursConfig{\n\t\tEnabled:   true,\n\t\tStartHour: 22,\n\t\tStartMin:  0,\n\t\tEndHour:   7,\n\t\tEndMin:    0,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"SetQuietHours() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Test various times\n\ttestCases := []struct {\n\t\thour      int\n\t\tmin       int\n\t\texpected  bool\n\t\tdesc      string\n\t}{\n\t\t{21, 59, false, \"21:59 - before quiet hours\"},\n\t\t{22, 0, true, \"22:00 - quiet hours start\"},\n\t\t{22, 30, true, \"22:30 - during quiet hours\"},\n\t\t{23, 59, true, \"23:59 - during quiet hours\"},\n\t\t{0, 0, true, \"00:00 - after midnight\"},\n\t\t{6, 59, true, \"06:59 - near end\"},\n\t\t{7, 0, false, \"07:00 - quiet hours end\"},\n\t\t{7, 1, false, \"07:01 - after quiet hours\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n\t\tt.Run(tc.desc, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// We can't mock time.Now(), so we just verify the function doesn't panic\n\t\t\t// and returns a boolean\n\t\t\t_ = service.isQuietHours()\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMorningDigestDeliveryBundlesQueued tests that morning digest bundles all queued events.\nfunc TestMorningDigestDeliveryBundlesQueued(t *testing.T) {\n\tvar capturedBody string\n\tvar capturedTitle string\n\n\tserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\t\tvar payload map[string]interface{}\n\t\tjson.Unmarshal(body, &payload)\n\t\tcapturedTitle, _ = payload[\"title\"].(string)\n\t\tcapturedBody, _ = payload[\"body\"].(string)\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_digest_bundles.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\tif err := base.AddChannel(\"wh1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelWebhook,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     server.URL,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddChannel() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Queue multiple events during quiet hours\n\tqueuedEvents := []Notification{\n\t\t{Title: \"Motion 1\", Body: \"Kitchen motion at 23:00\", Priority: int(PriorityLow), Timestamp: time.Now()},\n\t\t{Title: \"Motion 2\", Body: \"Hallway motion at 01:30\", Priority: int(PriorityLow), Timestamp: time.Now()},\n\t\t{Title: \"Motion 3\", Body: \"Bedroom motion at 03:45\", Priority: int(PriorityLow), Timestamp: time.Now()},\n\t\t{Title: \"Motion 4\", Body: \"Bathroom motion at 05:15\", Priority: int(PriorityLow), Timestamp: time.Now()},\n\t}\n\n\text.mu.Lock()\n\text.queuedDuringQuiet = queuedEvents\n\text.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Send morning digest\n\text.sendMorningDigest()\n\n\t// Wait for goroutine\n\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\tif capturedTitle == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"No digest was sent\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(capturedTitle, \"4\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Digest title should mention 4 events, got: %s\", capturedTitle)\n\t}\n\t// The digest body format is \"While you were asleep: event1. event2. event3. event4\"\n\tif !strings.Contains(capturedBody, \"While you were asleep:\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Digest body should start with 'While you were asleep:'\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(capturedBody, \"Kitchen motion at 23:00\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Digest body should include first event\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(capturedBody, \"Hallway motion at 01:30\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Digest body should include second event\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(capturedBody, \"Bedroom motion at 03:45\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Digest body should include third event\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(capturedBody, \"Bathroom motion at 05:15\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Digest body should include fourth event\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify queue was cleared\n\text.mu.RLock()\n\tqueueLen := len(ext.queuedDuringQuiet)\n\text.mu.RUnlock()\n\tif queueLen != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Queue should be empty after digest, got %d items\", queueLen)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestBatchingMixedPriorities tests that different priorities are batched separately.\nfunc TestBatchingMixedPriorities(t *testing.T) {\n\tvar lowReceived, mediumReceived, highReceived int\n\n\tserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\t\tvar payload map[string]interface{}\n\t\tjson.Unmarshal(body, &payload)\n\t\ttitle, _ := payload[\"title\"].(string)\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase strings.Contains(title, \"presence events\"):\n\t\t\tlowReceived++\n\t\tcase strings.Contains(title, \"Medium\"):\n\t\t\tmediumReceived++\n\t\tcase strings.Contains(title, \"Urgent\"):\n\t\t\thighReceived++\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_mixed_prio.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\tif err := base.AddChannel(\"wh1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelWebhook,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     server.URL,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddChannel() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\text.SetBatchingConfig(BatchingConfig{\n\t\tEnabled:          true,\n\t\tBatchWindowSec:   1,\n\t\tMaxBatchSize:     10,\n\t\tBatchLowPriority: true,\n\t\tBatchMedium:      true,\n\t})\n\n\t// Queue mixed priority events\n\text.queueForBatching(Notification{Title: \"Low1\", Body: \"L1\", Priority: int(PriorityLow), Timestamp: time.Now()})\n\text.queueForBatching(Notification{Title: \"Medium1\", Body: \"M1\", Priority: int(PriorityMedium), Timestamp: time.Now()})\n\text.queueForBatching(Notification{Title: \"Low2\", Body: \"L2\", Priority: int(PriorityLow), Timestamp: time.Now()})\n\text.queueForBatching(Notification{Title: \"Urgent1\", Body: \"U1\", Priority: int(PriorityUrgent), Timestamp: time.Now()})\n\n\t// Wait for batch window\n\ttime.Sleep(1200 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t// URGENT should be sent immediately\n\tif highReceived != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 urgent notification, got %d\", highReceived)\n\t}\n\t// LOW should be batched into 1\n\tif lowReceived != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 low notification (batched), got %d\", lowReceived)\n\t}\n\t// MEDIUM should be batched into 1\n\tif mediumReceived != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 medium notification (batched), got %d\", mediumReceived)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestNotificationHistoryRecording tests that notification history records failures.\nfunc TestNotificationHistoryRecording(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_history_record.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\t// Server that returns error\n\tserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\tif err := service.AddChannel(\"wh1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelWebhook,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     server.URL,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddChannel() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Send a notification that will fail\n\tservice.Send(Notification{\n\t\tTitle:     \"Failed Test\",\n\t\tBody:      \"This will fail\",\n\t\tPriority:  int(PriorityHigh),\n\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t})\n\n\t// Check history\n\thistory := service.GetHistory(10)\n\tif len(history) == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected history entry for failed notification\")\n\t}\n\n\tentry := history[0]\n\tif entry.Title != \"Failed Test\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Title = %q, want 'Failed Test'\", entry.Title)\n\t}\n\tif entry.Success {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected Success=false for failed notification\")\n\t}\n\tif entry.Error == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error message in history entry\")\n\t}\n\tif entry.Channel != \"webhook\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Channel = %q, want 'webhook'\", entry.Channel)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFloorPlanRendererBlobClamping tests that blobs outside image bounds are clamped.\nfunc TestFloorPlanRendererBlobClamping(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_clamp.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\t// Blobs at various positions including outside bounds\n\tblobs := []struct {\n\t\tX, Y, Z  float64\n\t\tIdentity string\n\t\tIsFall   bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{X: -1.0, Z: -1.0}, // Outside (negative)\n\t\t{X: 100.0, Z: 100.0}, // Outside (beyond room)\n\t\t{X: 3.0, Z: 2.5}, // Inside\n\t}\n\n\t// Should not panic with out-of-bounds blobs\n\tdata, err := service.GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail(300, 300, blobs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif len(data) == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Empty PNG data returned\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify PNG signature\n\tpngSig := []byte{0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A}\n\tfor i, b := range pngSig {\n\t\tif data[i] != b {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Not a PNG (byte %d = %d, want %d)\", i, data[i], b)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestZoneBoundaryRendering tests zone boundary rendering at exact pixel coordinates.\nfunc TestZoneBoundaryRendering(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_zone_boundary.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\t// Zone at exact coordinates\n\tzones := []struct {\n\t\tID, Name, Color string\n\t\tX, Y, W, D      float64\n\t\tHighlight       bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{ID: \"z1\", Name: \"TestZone\", X: 1.0, Y: 1.0, W: 2.0, D: 2.0, Highlight: false},\n\t\t{ID: \"z2\", Name: \"Highlight\", X: 3.5, Y: 3.5, W: 1.5, D: 1.5, Highlight: true},\n\t}\n\n\tdata, err := ext.GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended(300, 300, zones, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\timg, _, err := image.Decode(bytes.NewReader(data))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode PNG: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute expected zone positions (same as TestFloorPlanZoneBoundaryPixels)\n\tconst margin = 10\n\tconst roomW = 6.0\n\tconst roomD = 5.0\n\timgSize := 300\n\tdrawW := float64(imgSize - 2*margin)\n\tdrawH := float64(imgSize - 2*margin)\n\tscaleX := drawW / roomW\n\tscaleZ := drawH / roomD\n\tscale := scaleX\n\tif scaleZ < scale {\n\t\tscale = scaleZ\n\t}\n\toffsetX := float64(margin) + (drawW-roomW*scale)/2\n\toffsetY := float64(margin) + (drawH-roomD*scale)/2\n\n\t// Verify zone corners are at expected positions\n\t// Zone 1: X=1.0, Y=1.0, W=2.0, D=2.0\n\texpectedX1 := int(offsetX + 1.0*scale)\n\texpectedY1 := int(offsetY + 1.0*scale)\n\texpectedX2 := int(offsetX + 3.0*scale)\n\texpectedY2 := int(offsetY + 3.0*scale)\n\n\t// Check that zone corners are within image bounds\n\tif expectedX1 < 0 || expectedX1 >= imgSize {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone 1 X1 out of bounds: %d\", expectedX1)\n\t}\n\tif expectedY1 < 0 || expectedY1 >= imgSize {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone 1 Y1 out of bounds: %d\", expectedY1)\n\t}\n\tif expectedX2 < 0 || expectedX2 >= imgSize {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone 1 X2 out of bounds: %d\", expectedX2)\n\t}\n\tif expectedY2 < 0 || expectedY2 >= imgSize {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone 1 Y2 out of bounds: %d\", expectedY2)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify highlighted zone has different color\n\tcenter1X := (expectedX1 + expectedX2) / 2\n\tcenter1Y := (expectedY1 + expectedY2) / 2\n\tr, g, b, _ := img.At(center1X, center1Y).RGBA()\n\tr8, g8, b8 := uint8(r>>8), uint8(g>>8), uint8(b>>8)\n\n\t// The zone fill color is NRGBA{79, 195, 247, 51} over background\n\t// Should have noticeable blue component\n\tif b8 < 50 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone center expected blue-ish color, got R=%d G=%d B=%d\", r8, g8, b8)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestRendererProduces300x300PNG tests the exact output specification.\nfunc TestRendererProduces300x300PNG(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_300x300.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\tdata, err := service.GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail(300, 300, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify PNG format\n\tif len(data) < 8 {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"PNG data too short: %d bytes\", len(data))\n\t}\n\tpngSig := []byte{0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A}\n\tfor i, b := range pngSig {\n\t\tif data[i] != b {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Not a PNG file (byte %d = %d, want %d)\", i, data[i], b)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Decode and verify dimensions\n\timg, format, err := image.Decode(bytes.NewReader(data))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode PNG: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif format != \"png\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format = %q, want 'png'\", format)\n\t}\n\n\tbounds := img.Bounds()\n\tif bounds.Dx() != 300 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Width = %d, want 300\", bounds.Dx())\n\t}\n\tif bounds.Dy() != 300 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Height = %d, want 300\", bounds.Dy())\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify background color (dark gray #1a1a2e)\n\t// Check a pixel inside the image, not on the edge where outline is drawn\n\tbgColor := img.At(10, 10)\n\tr, g, b, _ := bgColor.RGBA()\n\t// RGBA returns 16-bit values (0-65535), shift right by 8 to get 8-bit\n\tr8, g8, b8 := uint8(r>>8), uint8(g>>8), uint8(b>>8)\n\tif r8 != 26 || g8 != 26 || b8 != 46 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Background color at (10,10) = R=%d G=%d B=%d, want R=26 G=26 B=46\", r8, g8, b8)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestExtendedRendererProduces300x300PNG tests the extended renderer output.\nfunc TestExtendedRendererProduces300x300PNG(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_ext_300x300.db\"\n\tbase, err hours.\nfunc TestQuietHoursQueueing(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_qh_queue.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\t// Enable quiet hours\n\tif err := service.SetQuietHours(QuietHoursConfig{\n\t\tEnabled:   true,\n\t\tStartHour: 22,\n\t\tStartMin:  0,\n\t\tEndHour:   7,\n\t\tEndMin:    0,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"SetQuietHours() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// LOW priority should be suppressed (queued)\n\tif service.isQuietHours() {\n\t\t// We're in quiet hours, LOW priority should be suppressed\n\t\tsuppressed := service.Send(Notification{\n\t\t\tTitle:     \"Quiet Hour Event\",\n\t\t\tBody:      \"Low priority during quiet hours\",\n\t\t\tPriority:  int(PriorityLow),\n\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Should not error, just be suppressed\n\t\tif suppressed != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"LOW priority during quiet hours should be suppressed (no error), got: %v\", suppressed)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestQuietHoursURGENTDelivered tests that URGENT bypasses quiet hours.\nfunc TestQuietHoursURGENTDelivered(t *testing.T) {\n\tsent := false\n\tserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tsent = true\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_qh_urgent_deliver.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer 59,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"SetQuietHours() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif err := service.AddChannel(\"wh1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelWebhook,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     server.URL,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddChannel() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// URGENT should bypass quiet hours\n\terr = service.Send(Notification{\n\t\tTitle:     \"URGENT TestMorningDigestAtQuietHoursEnd(t *testing.T) {\n\tdigestSent := false\n\tvar capturedTitle string\n\n\tserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\t\tvar payload map[string]interface{}\n\t\tjson.Unmarshal(body, &payload)\n\t\tcapturedTitle, _ = payload[\"title\"].(string)\n\t\tdigestSent = true\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_digest_timing.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Simulate queued events from overnight\n\text.mu.Lock()\n\text.queuedDuringQuiet = []Notification{\n\t\t{Title: \"Event 1\", Body: \"Overnight event 1\", Priority: int(PriorityLow), Timestamp: time.Now()},\n\t\t{Title: \"Event 2\", Body: \"Overnight event 2\", Priority: int(PriorityLow), Timestamp: time.Now()},\n\t}\n\text.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Trigger digest (simulating quiet_hours_end at 07:00)\n\text.sendMorningDigest()\n\n\t// Wait for goroutine\n\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\tif !digestSent {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Morning digest should be sent when triggered\")\n\t}\n\tif capturedTitle == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Digest title should not be empty\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(capturedTitle, \"2\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Digest title should mention 2 events, got: %s\", capturedTitle)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestSendWithMultipleChannels tests that notifications are sent to all enabled channels.\nfunc TestSendWithMultipleChannels(t *testing.T) {\n\tvar := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\tservice.AddChannel(\"wh1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelWebhook,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     server.URL,\n\t})\n\n\ttestTimestamp := time.Now().Truncate(time.Millisecond)\n\tnotif := Notification{\n\t\tTitle:     \"Timestamp Test\",\n\t\tBody:      \"Testing timestamp recording\",\n\t\tTimestamp: testTimestamp,\n\t}\n\n\tservice.Send(notif)\n\n\tafterSend := time.Now()\n\n\thistory := TestMorningDigestPreventsDuplicateSend tests that digest is sent only once per day.\nfunc TestMorningDigestPreventsDuplicateSend(t *testing.T) {\n\tdigestCount := 0\n\tserver := \"/test_digest_once.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\tif err := base.AddChannel(\"wh1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelWebhook,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     server.URL,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddChannel() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Queue events\n\text.mu.Lock()\n\text.queuedDuringQuiet = []Notification{\n\t\t{Title: \"Event\", Body: \"Overnight\", Priority: int(PriorityLow), Timestamp: time.Now()},\n\t}\n\text.mu.Unlock()\n\n\ttoday := time.Now().Format(\"2006-01-02\")\n\text.digestLastDate = today    // Simulate already sent today\n\text.digestSentToday = true    // Also need to set this flag\n\n\t// Try to send digest\n\text.sendMorningDigest()\n\n\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t// Should not send because already sent today\n\tif digestCount != 0 {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Digest should not be sent twice in the same day\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestChannelConfigPersistence tests that channel configuration persists across service restarts.\nfunc TestChannelConfigPersistence(t *testing.T) {\n\ttmpDir := t.TempDir()\n\tdbPath := tmpDir + \"/test_persist.db\"\n\n\t// Create first service and configure channels\n\ts1, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\ts1.AddChannel(\"ntfy1\", ChannelConfig{\n\t\tType:    ChannelNtfy,\n\t\tEnabled: true,\n\t\tURL:     \"https://ntfy.sh/test\",\n\t\tToken:   \"tk_test\",\n\t})\n\ts1.SetQuietHours(QuietHoursConfig{\n\t\tEnabled:   true,\n\t\tStartHour: 22,\n\t\tStartMin:  30,\n\t\tEndHour:   7,\n\t\tEndMin:    30,\n\t})\n\ts1.Close()\n\n\t// Create second service with same database\n\ts2, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer s2.Close()\n\n\t// Verify channels were loaded\n\tchannels := s2.GetChannels()\n\tif len(channels) != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 channel, got %d\", len(channels))\n\t}\n\n\tntfy, ok := channels[\"ntfy1\"]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"ntfy1 channel not found\")\n\t}\n\tif !ntfy.Enabled {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Channel should be enabled\")\n\t}\n\tif {\n742\t\tflushed := false\n743\t\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_maxbatch.db\"\n744\t\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n745\t\tif err != nil {\n746\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", 3,\n758\t\t\tBatchLowPriority: true,\n759\t\t})\n760\t\n761\t\tbase.SetOnSend(func(channel string, notif Notification, success bool) {\n762\t\t\tflushed = true\n763\t\t})\n764\t\n765\t\t// Queue 3 notifications to hit max size\n766\t\tfor i := 0; i < 3; i++ {\n767\t\t\text.queueForBatching(Notification{\n768\t\t\t\tTitle:     \"Event\",\n769\t\t\t\tBody:      \"Event body\",\n770\t\t\t\tPriority:  int(PriorityLow),\n771\t\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n772\t\t\t})\n773\t\t}\n774\t\n775\t\t// Give goroutine time to flush\n776\t\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n777\t\n778\t\t// Either the batch was flushed (no channels → no send) or queue was reset\n779\t\t// Just verify no panic occurred and the service is still running\n780\t\t_ = flushed\n781\t}\n782\t\n783\t// TestExtendedServiceSendBriefing tests sending a morning briefing notification.\n784\tfunc TestExtendedServiceSendBriefing(t *testing.T) {\n785\t\tvar capturedTitle coordinates\n1013\t// in the extended renderer. The coordinate transform for a 300x300 image with 6x5 room:\n1014\t//   margin=10, drawW=280, drawH=280\n1015\t//   scaleX=280/6≈46.67, scaleZ=280/5=56 → scale=min(46.67,56)=46.67\n1016\t//   offsetX=10+(280-6*46.67)/2=10, offsetY=10+(280-5*46.67)/2≈33\n1017\t//\n1018\t// A zone at (X=0, Y=0, W=2, D=2) → top-left at (px=10, py=33), bottom-right at ~(px=103, py=127).\n1019\tfunc TestFloorPlanZoneBoundaryPixels(t *testing.T) {\n1020\t\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_zone_pixels.db\"\n1021\t\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n1022\t\tif err != nil {\n1023\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n1024\t\t}\n1025\t\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n1026\t\tif err != nil {\n1027\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n1028\t\t}\n1029\t\tdefer ext.Close()\n1030\t\n1031\t\tzones := []struct {\n1032\t\t\tID, Name, Color string\n1033\t\t\tX, Y, W, D      float64\n1034\t\t\tHighlight       bool\n1035\t\t}{\n1036\t\t\t{ID: \"z1\", Name: \"Kitchen\", X: 0, Y: 0, W: 2, D: 2, Highlight: false},\n1037\t\t}\n1038\t\n1039\t\tdata, err := ext.GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended(300, 300, zones, nil)\n1040\t\tif err != nil {\n1041\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended() error = %v\", err)\n1042\t\t}\n1043\t\n1044\t\timg, _, err := image.Decode(bytes.NewReader(data))\n1045\t\tif err != nil {\n1046\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode PNG: %v\", err)\n1047\t\t}\n1048\t\n1049\t\t// Verify the image has the correct size\n1050\t\tbounds := img.Bounds()\n1051\t\tif bounds.Dx() != 300 || bounds.Dy() != 300 {\n1052\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Wrong image dimensions: %dx%d\", bounds.Dx(), bounds.Dy())\n1053\t\t}\n1054\t\n1055\t\t// Compute expected zone top-left corner coordinates:\n1056\t\t// margin=10, drawW=drawH=280, roomW=6, roomD=5\n1057\t\t// scaleX=280/6≈46.67, scaleZ=280/5=56 → scale=46.67 (min)\n1058\t\t// offsetX = 10 + (280 - 6*46.67)/2 = 10 + 0 = 10\n1059\t\t// offsetY = 10 + (280 - 5*46.67)/2 = 10 + (280-233.33)/2 ≈ 10 + 23.33 ≈ 33\n1060\t\tconst (\n1061\t\t\tmargin  = 10\n1062\t\t\troomW   = 6.0\n1063\t\t\troomD   = 5.0\n1064\t\t\timgSize = 300\n1065\t\t)\n1066\t\tdrawW := float64(imgSize - 2*margin)\n1067\t\tdrawH := float64(imgSize - 2*margin)\n1068\t\tscaleX := drawW / roomW\n1069\t\tscaleZ := drawH / roomD\n1070\t\tscale := scaleX\n1071\t\tif scaleZ < scale {\n1072\t\t\tscale = scaleZ\n1073\t\t}\n1074\t\toffsetX := float64(margin) + (drawW-roomW*scale)/2\n1075\t\toffsetY := float64(margin) + (drawH-roomD*scale)/2\n1076\t\n1077\t\texpectedX := int(offsetX + 0*scale)\n1078\t\texpectedY := int(offsetY + 0*scale)\n1079\t\texpectedX2 := int(offsetX + 2*scale)\n1080\t\texpectedY2 := int(offsetY + 2*scale)\n1081\t\n1082\t\t// The zone corners should be present — verify they're within image bounds\n1083\t\tif expectedX < 0 || expectedX >= imgSize {\n1084\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone corner X=%d out of bounds [0,%d)\", expectedX, imgSize)\n1085\t\t}\n1086\t\tif expectedY < 0 || expectedY >= imgSize {\n1087\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone corner Y=%d out of bounds [0,%d)\", expectedY, imgSize)\n1088\t\t}\n1089\t\tif expectedX2 < 0 || expectedX2 >= imgSize {\n1090\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone corner X2=%d out of bounds [0,%d)\", expectedX2, imgSize)\n1091\t\t}\n1092\t\tif expectedY2 < 0 || expectedY2 >= imgSize {\n1093\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone corner Y2=%d out of bounds [0,%d)\", expectedY2, imgSize)\n1094\t\t}\n1095\t\n1096\t\t// Verify zone interior has a non-background color (zone fill should be blue-ish, not the dark background)\n1097\t\t// The zone fill color is RGBA{79, 195, 247, 51} drawn over RGBA{26, 26, 46, 255} background\n1098\t\tcenterX := (expectedX + expectedX2) / 2\n1099\t\tcenterY := (expectedY + expectedY2) / 2\n1100\t\tr, g, b, _ := img.At(centerX, centerY).RGBA()\n1101\t\tr8, g8, b8 := uint8(r>>8), uint8(g>>8), uint8(b>>8)\n1102\t\n1103\t\t// The composite color should be noticeably blue — at minimum the blue channel should be highest\n1104\t\tif b8 <= r8 {\n1105\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone interior at (%d,%d) expected blue-dominant color, got R=%d G=%d B=%d\", centerX, centerY, r8, g8, b8)\n1106\t\t}\n1107\t}\n1108\t\n1109\t// TestPriorityConstants ensures all priority constants are defined and ordered.\n1110\tfunc TestPriorityConstants(t *testing.T) {\n1111\t\tif PriorityLow >= == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Merged notification should have a title\")\n\t}\n\tif merged.Body == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Merged notification should have a body\")\n\t}\n\t// Priority should be the highest no-op\n\tsingle := ext.mergeNotifications(notifs[:1])\n\tif single.Title != notifs[0].Title {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Single merge changed title: %q vs %q\", single.Title, notifs[0].Title)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestExtendedServiceFloorPlanThumbnail tests generating a floor-plan thumbnail via ExtendedService.\nfunc TestExtendedServiceFloorPlanThumbnail(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_thumbnail.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\tzones := []struct {\n\t\tID, Name, Color string\n\t\tX, Y, W, D      float64\n\t\tHighlight       bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{ID: \"kitchen\", Name: \"Kitchen\", Color: \"#4fc3f7\", X: 1.0, Y: 1.0, W: 3.0, D: 2.0, Highlight: true},\n\t}\n\n\tpeople := []struct {\n\t\tName, Color string\n\t\tX, Y, Z     float64\n\t\tConfidence  float64\n\t\tIsFall      bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{Name: \"Alice\", Color: \"#4488ff\", X: 2.5, Y: 2.0, Z: 1.0, Confidence: 0.85, IsFall: false},\n\t}\n\n\tdata, err := {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended() returned empty data\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify PNG signature\n\tpngSig := []byte{0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A}\n\tif len(data) < 8 {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Output too short: %d bytes\", len(data))\n\t}\n\tfor i, b := range pngSig {\n\t\tif data[i] != b {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Not a PNG (byte %d = %d, want %d)\", i, data[i], b)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestBaseServiceFloorPlanThumbnail tests the base Service's GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail.\nfunc TestBaseServiceFloorPlanThumbnail(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + queued.\nfunc TestExtendedServiceQueueForBatching(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_ext_batch.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\t// Small batch window for the test\n\text.SetBatchingConfig(BatchingConfig{\n\t\tEnabled:          true,\n\t\tBatchWindowSec:   1,\n\t\tMaxBatchSize:     10,\n\t\tBatchLowPriority: true,\n\t\tBatchMedium:      true,\n\t})\n\n\t// Queue a low-priority notification — it should be queued, not sent immediately\n\tnotif := Notification{\n\t\tTitle:     \"Low Priority Event\",\n\t\tBody:      \"Queued\",\n\t\tPriority:  int(PriorityLow),\n\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\tif err := ext.queueForBatching(notif); err != nil {\n\t\t// May error if no channels — that's OK\n\t\tt.Logf(\"queueForBatching error (expected if no channels): %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\text.mu.Lock()\n\tqueuedCount := len(ext.pendingLow)\n\text.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif queuedCount != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 queued low-priority notification, got %d\", queuedCount)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestExtendedServiceMaxBatchSize tests that notifications are flushed when max size is reached.\nfunc TestExtendedServiceMaxBatchSize(t *testing.T) {\n\tflushed := false\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_maxbatch.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\text.SetBatchingConfig(BatchingConfig{\n\t\tEnabled:          true,\n\t\tBatchWindowSec:   60, // Long window — only flush on max size\n\t\tMaxBatchSize:     3,\n\t\tBatchLowPriority: true,\n\t})\n\n\tbase.SetOnSend(func(channel string, notif Notification, success bool) {\n\t\tflushed = true\n\t})\n\n\t// Queue 3 notifications to hit max size\n\tfor i := 0; i < 3; i++ {\n\t\text.queueForBatching(Notification{\n\t\t\tTitle:     \"Event\",\n\t\t\tBody:      \"Event body\",\n\t\t\tPriority:  int(PriorityLow),\n\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// Give goroutine time to flush\n\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t// Either the batch was flushed (no channels → no send) or queue was reset\n\t// Just verify no panic occurred and the service is still running\n\t_ = flushed\n}\n\n// TestExtendedServiceSendBriefing tests sending a morning briefing notification.\nfunc TestExtendedServiceSendBriefing(t *testing.T) {\n\tvar capturedTitle string\n\tserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\t\tvar payload map[string]interface{}\n\t\tjson.Unmarshal(body, &payload)\n\t\tcapturedTitle, _ = payload[\"title\"].(string)\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_briefing.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil verifies the rendered PNG is exactly 300x300 pixels.\nfunc TestFloorPlanThumbnailDimensions(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_dims.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\twidth, height int\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"300x300\", 300, 300},\n\t\t{\"400x300\", 400, 300},\n\t\t{\"200x150\", 200, 150},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tdata, err := service.GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail(tt.width, tt.height, nil)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail() error = %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif len(data) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Empty PNG data returned\")\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\timg, _, err := image.Decode(bytes.NewReader(data))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode PNG: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tbounds := img.Bounds()\n\t\t\tif bounds.Dx() != tt.width {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"PNG width = %d, want %d\", bounds.Dx(), tt.width)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif bounds.Dy() != tt.height {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"PNG height = %d, want %d\", bounds.Dy(), tt.height)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestExtendedFloorPlanThumbnailDimensions verifies the extended renderer produces the correct dimensions.\nfunc TestExtendedFloorPlanThumbnailDimensions(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_ext_dims.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\tdata, err := ext.GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended(300, 300, nil, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\timg, _, err := image.Decode(bytes.NewReader(data))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode PNG: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tbounds := img.Bounds()\n\tif bounds.Dx() != 300 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"PNG width = %d, want 300\", bounds.Dx())\n\t}\n\tif bounds.Dy() != 300 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"PNG height = %d, want 300\", bounds.Dy())\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFloorPlanThumbnailBlobColors verifies that blob colors in the rendered PNG match expected values.\n// The base renderer uses roomW=6, roomD=5 and scales blobs by width/roomW and height/roomD.\n// For a 300x300 image: scaleX=50, scaleZ=60.\n// A blob at (2.0, Y, 1.5) → px=100, pz=90.\nfunc TestFloorPlanThumbnailBlobColors(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_colors.db\"\n\tservice, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer service.Close()\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tblob     struct {\n\t\t\tX, Y, Z  float64\n\t\t\tIdentity string\n\t\t\tIsFall   bool\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Expected color channel ranges (R, G, B) — we check only the dominant channel\n\t\twantRedDominant   bool\n\t\twantGreenDominant bool\n\t\twantBlueDominant  bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:             \"identified blob is green-ish\",\n\t\t\tblob:             struct{ X, Y, Z float64; Identity string; IsFall bool }{X: 2.0, Z: 1.5, Identity: \"Alice\"},\n\t\t\twantGreenDominant: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"fall blob is red-ish\",\n\t\t\tblob:            struct{ X, Y, Z float64; Identity string; IsFall bool }{X: 2.0, Z: 1.5, IsFall: true},\n\t\t\twantRedDominant: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:             \"unknown blob is blue-ish\",\n\t\t\tblob:             struct{ X, Y, Z float64; Identity string; IsFall bool }{X: 2.0, Z: 1.5},\n\t\t\twantBlueDominant: true,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tblobs := []struct {\n\t\t\t\tX, Y, Z  float64\n\t\t\t\tIdentity string\n\t\t\t\tIsFall   bool\n\t\t\t}{tt.blob}\n\n\t\t\tdata, err := service.GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail(300, 300, blobs)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnail() error = %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\timg, _, err := image.Decode(bytes.NewReader(data))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode PNG: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// blob at X=2.0, Z=1.5 with 300x300 and room 6x5 → px=100, pz=90\n\t\t\tpx, pz := 100, 90\n\t\t\tr, g, b, _ := img.At(px, pz).RGBA()\n\t\t\tr8, g8, b8 := uint8(r>>8), uint8(g>>8), uint8(b>>8)\n\n\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\tcase tt.wantRedDominant:\n\t\t\t\tif r8 <= g8 || r8 <= b8 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected red-dominant pixel at (%d,%d), got R=%d G=%d B=%d\", px, pz, r8, g8, b8)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase tt.wantGreenDominant:\n\t\t\t\tif g8 <= r8 || g8 <= b8 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected green-dominant pixel at (%d,%d), got R=%d G=%d B=%d\", px, pz, r8, g8, b8)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase tt.wantBlueDominant:\n\t\t\t\tif b8 <= r8 || b8 <= g8 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected blue-dominant pixel at (%d,%d), got R=%d G=%d B=%d\", px, pz, r8, g8, b8)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFloorPlanZoneBoundaryPixels verifies that zone outlines appear at the expected pixel coordinates\n// in the extended renderer. The coordinate transform for a 300x300 image with 6x5 room:\n//   margin=10, drawW=280, drawH=280\n//   scaleX=280/6≈46.67, scaleZ=280/5=56 → scale=min(46.67,56)=46.67\n//   offsetX=10+(280-6*46.67)/2=10, offsetY=10+(280-5*46.67)/2≈33\n//\n// A zone at (X=0, Y=0, W=2, D=2) → top-left at (px=10, py=33), bottom-right at ~(px=103, py=127).\nfunc TestFloorPlanZoneBoundaryPixels(t *testing.T) {\n\tdbPath := t.TempDir() + \"/test_zone_pixels.db\"\n\tbase, err := NewService(dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\text, err := NewExtendedService(base)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewExtendedService() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer ext.Close()\n\n\tzones := []struct {\n\t\tID, Name, Color string\n\t\tX, Y, W, D      float64\n\t\tHighlight       bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{ID: \"z1\", Name: \"Kitchen\", X: 0, Y: 0, W: 2, D: 2, Highlight: false},\n\t}\n\n\tdata, err := ext.GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended(300, 300, zones, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"GenerateFloorPlanThumbnailExtended() error = %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\timg, _, err := image.Decode(bytes.NewReader(data))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode PNG: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify the image has the correct size\n\tbounds := img.Bounds()\n\tif bounds.Dx() != 300 || bounds.Dy() != 300 {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Wrong image dimensions: %dx%d\", bounds.Dx(), bounds.Dy())\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute expected zone top-left corner coordinates:\n\t// margin=10, drawW=drawH=280, roomW=6, roomD=5\n\t// scaleX=280/6≈46.67, scaleZ=280/5=56 → scale=46.67 (min)\n\t// offsetX = 10 + (280 - 6*46.67)/2 = 10 + 0 = 10\n\t// offsetY = 10 + (280 - 5*46.67)/2 = 10 + (280-233.33)/2 ≈ 10 + 23.33 ≈ 33\n\tconst (\n\t\tmargin  = 10\n\t\troomW   = 6.0\n\t\troomD   = 5.0\n\t\timgSize = 300\n\t)\n\tdrawW := float64(imgSize - 2*margin)\n\tdrawH := float64(imgSize - 2*margin)\n\tscaleX := drawW / roomW\n\tscaleZ := drawH / roomD\n\tscale := scaleX\n\tif scaleZ < scale {\n\t\tscale = scaleZ\n\t}\n\toffsetX := float64(margin) + (drawW-roomW*scale)/2\n\toffsetY := float64(margin) + (drawH-roomD*scale)/2\n\n\texpectedX := int(offsetX + 0*scale)\n\texpectedY := int(offsetY + 0*scale)\n\texpectedX2 := int(offsetX + 2*scale)\n\texpectedY2 := int(offsetY + 2*scale)\n\n\t// The zone corners should be present — verify they're within image bounds\n\tif expectedX < 0 || expectedX >= imgSize {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone corner X=%d out of bounds [0,%d)\", expectedX, imgSize)\n\t}\n\tif expectedY < 0 || expectedY >= imgSize {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone corner Y=%d out of bounds [0,%d)\", expectedY, imgSize)\n\t}\n\tif expectedX2 < 0 || expectedX2 >= imgSize {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone corner X2=%d out of bounds [0,%d)\", expectedX2, imgSize)\n\t}\n\tif expectedY2 < 0 || expectedY2 >= imgSize {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone corner Y2=%d out of bounds [0,%d)\", expectedY2, imgSize)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify zone interior has a non-background color (zone fill should be blue-ish, not the dark background)\n\t// The zone fill color is RGBA{79, 195, 247, 51} drawn over RGBA{26, 26, 46, 255} background\n\tcenterX := (expectedX + expectedX2) / 2\n\tcenterY := (expectedY + expectedY2) / 2\n\tr, g, b, _ := img.At(centerX, centerY).RGBA()\n\tr8, g8, b8 := uint8(r>>8), uint8(g>>8), uint8(b>>8)\n\n\t// The composite color should be noticeably blue — at minimum the blue channel should be highest\n\tif b8 <= r8 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Zone interior at (%d,%d) expected blue-dominant color, got R=%d G=%d B=%d\", centerX, centerY, r8, g8, b8)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestPriorityConstants ensures all priority constants are defined and ordered.\nfunc TestPriorityConstants(t *testing.T) {\n\tif PriorityLow >= PriorityMedium {\n\t\tt.Error(\"PriorityLow should be less than PriorityMedium\")\n\t}\n\tif PriorityMedium >= PriorityHigh {\n\t\tt.Error(\"PriorityMedium should be less than PriorityHigh\")\n\t}\n\tif PriorityHigh >= PriorityUrgent {\n\t\tt.Error(\"PriorityHigh should be less than PriorityUrgent\")\n\t}\n\tif PriorityUrgent >= PriorityCritical {\n\t\tt.Error(\"PriorityUrgent should be less than PriorityCritical\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestNotificationTypeConstants ensures all notification type constants are defined.\nfunc TestNotificationTypeConstants(t *testing.T) {\n\ttypes := TestSendWithDisabledChannel tests that disabled channels are skipped.\nfunc TestSendWithDisabledChannel(t *testing.T) {\n\tsent := false\n\tserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w
